issue_title: [quarkus-next] Missing comment generated by Liquibase executor in the custom script
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

Related to all DB vendors.

After upgrade to Liquibase 4.33.0 the `Create Database Change Log Table` message is missing from the custom-generated script as seen here: https://github.com/liquibase/liquibase/blob/v4.33.0/liquibase-standard/src/main/java/liquibase/changelog/StandardChangeLogHistoryService.java#L262

However, the log message should be present in the log, so we should be sure the branch is executed.

Not sure if it worth the effort to analyze what has changed since the Liquibase 4.29.1 -> 4.33.0 as the comment is not propagated to the executor and not written to the file. I think it should be only necessary to check if the log message i there.

EDIT: The DATABASECHANGELOG is always created in the database and outside of the custom Liquibase executor, so it seems user's executions are done separately than the default one done by Liquibase.

```
--------- org.keycloak.it.storage.database.dist.PostgreSQLDistTest output end  ---------
Error:  Tests run: 7, Failures: 1, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0, Time elapsed: 141.591 s <<< FAILURE! - in org.keycloak.it.storage.database.dist.PostgreSQLDistTest
Error: OR] org.keycloak.it.storage.database.dist.PostgreSQLDistTest.testKeycloakDbUpdateScript(CLIResult,RawDistRootPath)  Time elapsed: 18.304 s  <<< FAILURE!
-- java.lang.AssertionError: 
-- 
-- Expected: a string containing "Create Database Change Log Table"
--      but: was "-- *********************************************************************
-- -- * Keycloak database creation script - apply this script to empty DB *

--
```

Execute the DB tests

Like with other protocols, two coordinators should eventually merge.

Reported in #41290, and investigated in #41291 unit test.

issue_title: Access to user details for restricted admin fails after enabling organizationin realm
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

1 147 / 5 000
We have users with restricted administration rights on the realm.

In fact, these administrators have the following rights:
realm-management:query-clients, manage-users, query-users, view-realm, view-users, view-clients.

Since we enabled "organizations" on the realm, we have noticed that these administrators can no longer view a user's details.

In fact, a request to https://<keycloak_url>/auth/admin/realms/<realm>/organizations/?first=0&max=1 is triggered at least when accessing a user's details, and the result is a 403 Forbidden error!

By disabling "organizations" on the realm, these administrators regain the ability to view a user's details.

I haven't found any new rights dedicated to accessing organizations (query-organizations or view-organizations).

The only solution is to assign the "Manage-realm" role, which we don't want for these restricted users.
For example, we don't want that federation menu can be accessed in read/write mode for those restricted users.

Furthermore, I notice that "organizations" are present in the left-hand menu, and accessing this item produces the same error: 403 Forbidden.

Can you associate organization access with the "manage-users" role or something else to fix this regression?

Thanks in advance.
Envoyer des commentaires
Résultats de traduction disponibles 


### Version

26.0.5

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Access to all users details for a restricted administrator who have manage-users role even organizations are enabled

### Actual behavior

Forbidden access to organizations or users when trying to look at users details.

### How to Reproduce?

- Connect to Keycloak with a restricted user account with manage-users, query-users roles.
- Access to a user details in your realm  
- All is OK
- Activate organizations 
- Access to a user details in your realm  
- You should get 403 Forbidden error when keycloak tries accessing to organizations or click on organizations left menu.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Make it possible to check for permissions when deciding if a feature is enabled or not

Do not query organizations if manage-realm is not granted

Closes #41418

Signed-off-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>

issue_title: Parallel token exchange fails if client session is expired
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

token-exchange

### Describe the bug

I have experienced this in `token-exchange:v1`, but I think other areas might also be affected.
When exchanging a token from client-A into one from client-B, if there is an existing client session for client-B in the database and this session is expired, the `ClientSessionPersistentChangelogBasedTransaction.getSessionEntityFromPersister` will try to remove the existing session from the database. If the same token exchange is called twice in parallel, there is a high probability that one of them will fail with a `StaleObjectStateException` because the other one has already deleted the session.

I believe this failure can be avoided, the exception can just be ignored if the session has already been deleted from the database.

It does not matter if both calls end up on the same server (in a clustered environment) or not.

Tested in RHBK 26.2.5 and upstream 26.3.2 (both latest at the time of writing).


### Version

RHBK 26.2.5, 26.3.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

All token exchanges succeed

### Actual behavior

one of the exchanges fails with HTTP 500

### How to Reproduce?

For local testing, I reduced the client session timeouts to 30s idle / 1m max and ran the following script a couple of times with >30s pause in between:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

token="$(<get token for client-A>)"

curl -X POST -sS --location "http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/<realm>/protocol/openid-connect/token" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
    -d 'client_id=client-B&client_secret=client-B-secret&grant_type=urn%3Aietf%3Aparams%3Aoauth%3Agrant-type%3Atoken-exchange&requested_token_type=urn%3Aietf%3Aparams%3Aoauth%3Atoken-type%3Aaccess_token&subject_token='"$token" -i > curl1.txt &
curl -X POST -sS --location "http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/<realm>/protocol/openid-connect/token" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
    -d 'client_id=client-B&client_secret=client-B-secret&grant_type=urn%3Aietf%3Aparams%3Aoauth%3Agrant-type%3Atoken-exchange&requested_token_type=urn%3Aietf%3Aparams%3Aoauth%3Atoken-type%3Aaccess_token&subject_token='"$token" -i > curl2.txt &

wait

cat curl*.txt
```


### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Avoid deleting old client sessions

If this is done concurrently, this can load to errors with Hibernate and its optimistic locking

Closes #41427

Signed-off-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>

issue_title: Query parameter "claims" not forwarded to external provider
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

Keycloak can play the role of an Identity Broker and an external default OIDC 1.0 provider can be configured. Keycloak then forwards automatically known query parameters from the authorisation request to this configured provider (eg. "acr_values").

However, I've found that the query parameter "claims" is never forwarded. As the query parameter "claims" is also a standard OIDC query parameter, it is then expected that the query parameter "claims" is also forwarded automatically to the provider. However, it is not currently and, it is a blocking issue in order to use any configured OIDC 1.0 provider that requires this query parameter "claims".

### Version

26.x

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The query parameter "claims" is a standard OIDC query parameter and then, it must be forwarded automatically to the external provider as other standard OIDC query paramerters (eg. "acr_values").

### Actual behavior

The query parameter "claims" is not forwarded automatically to the external provider... even if it is configured within  the provider advanced configuration element "Forwarded query parameters". 

### How to Reproduce?

1/ Configure Keycloak as an Identity Broker.

2/ Add to Keycloak any OIDC 1.0 identity provider.

3/ Setup this identity provider as the default provider in the default authentication browser flow

4/ Add a client using the OIDC Auth Code Grant flow

5/ Initiate an Auth Code Grant Flow with the corresponding client id and add the "claims" query parameter with any valid value.

eg. claims= {"userinfo":{
  "name":null}}

6/ Verify in the network exchanges that the query parameter "claims" is unfortunately not forwarded to the external identity provider as expected

### Anything else?

Debugging Keycloak source code, I've discovered the root cause of this issue. In _org.keycloak.broker.oidc.OIDCIdentitProvider_ within the method _createAuthorizationUrl(AuthenticationRequest request)_ the query parameter "claims" is never added to the _jakarta.ws.rs.core.UriBuilder_ created in the same method from the parent class _org.keycloak.broker.oidc.AbstractOAuth2IdentitProvider_.

As a work around, I've tried also to add "claims" in the provider advanced configuration element "Forwarded query parameters". However, it doesn't work neither. Debugging Keycloak source code, I've discovered the root cause as well. The query parameter "claims" is not considered as an additional query parameter because it is one of the _KNOWN_REQ_PARAMS_ in _org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.request.AuthzEndpointRequestParser_. In _org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.AuthorizationEnpoint_ within method _updateAuthenticationSession()_, it is then not added as a client note with the key (_LOGIN_SESSION_NOTE_ADDITIONAL_REQ_PARAMS_PREFIX + paramName_) in the authentication session. Moreover, in _org.keycloak.broker.oidc.AbstractOAuth2IdentitProvider_ within method _createAuthorizationUrl(AuthenticationRequest request)_, forwarded query parameters are only added to the _jakarta.ws.rs.core.UriBuilder_ when available in the authentication session as a client note with the key (_AuthorizationEndpoint.LOGIN_SESSION_NOTE_ADDITIONAL_REQ_PARAMS_PREFIX + forwardParameter_) in the authentication session.
commit_message:
Allow forwarding parameters set as a client note in the authentication session

Closes #41670

Signed-off-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

When executing test classes with the annotation `@QuarkusTest` for `quarkus-next` branch and Quarkus deployment module, the surefire tries to fetch the same dependencies as did for the maven build of the module. It tries to obtain these artifacts from the https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/quarkus-next/pom.xml#L1721 sonatype repository.

issue_title: LDAP Sync all users takes unexpectedly long in 26.3 (> 30 min)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

When importing > 40k users from an Active Directory via LDAP, `Sync all users` which used to take about 90 seconds in Keycloak 26.2 takes more than 30 min in Keycloak 26.3.

No configuration changes were made other than upgrading from `26.2.4` to `26.3.2`. With two instances running side-by-side, one on 26.2, the other on 26.3, both connecting to the same AD, importing the same set of users, the difference in timing and CPU usage can be observed.

### Version

26.3.2

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

LDAP Sync all users to be as fast as in 26.2, about 90 seconds for 40k users. CPU usage at 50% during sync.

### Actual behavior

LDAP Sync all users for 40k users in 26.3 takes more than 30 min. CPU usage at 5% during sync.

### How to Reproduce?

- Have an LDAP directory containing a substantial number of users (40.000 in our environment)
- Configure LDAP User federation in Keycloak
- Initiate `Sync all users`

Observe that in 26.3, compared to in 26.2,
a) Sync all users takes much longer (30 min vs. 90 seconds)
b) CPU usage is much lower (5% vs. 50%)

### Anything else?

It is unclear whether this is a result of an intended change (as e. g. to reduce CPU load) or not, but 30 mins import time seems _really_ long.

Caches seem to behave differently between 26.2 and 26.3 during LDAP sync as seen in the `vendor_statistics` metrics. E. g., `vendor_statistics_misses` goes up for the `users` cache in 26.3 (not in 26.2). Not sure whether this is relevant, though.

Change e-mail verification to perform a find by UUID on LDAP only when the local and imported users are different

Closes #41532

Signed-off-by: Stefan Guilhen <sguilhen@redhat.com>

issue_title: Getting error 405 "Method Not Allowed" when calling the "certs" endpoint with HEAD method
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

There is a 405 error code when calling the "certs" endpoint (e.g. http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/certs) with HEAD http method on RHBK 26.

This issue wasn't happening on RHBK 22, as the endpoint was responding with 200 code.

### Version

Any Keycloak since 24 or newer

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

There should be a successful response to the HEAD request to the "certs" endpoint. Method `HEAD` should behave in a same way as method `GET` . It should just omit to return a body.

### Actual behavior

There is 405 error in response to the `HEAD` request to the `certs` endpoint (even if `GET` method works).

Some Keycloak endpoints are working with HEAD method (For example well-known endpoint http://localhost:8080/realms/master/.well-known/openid-configuration ). Some others are not. Some resteasy resource classes are broken, probably due the bug in quarkus resteasy-reactive .

### How to Reproduce?

- Start Keycloak server

- Run this request
`curl -vk http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/certs -I --http1.1`

- It works without parameter `-I` (which means calling regular `GET` instead of `HEAD`)

### Anything else?

The issue happens since Keycloak 24 when resteasy-reactive was introduced.

issue_title: RejectImplicitGrantExecutor does not return an error when a PAR request includes Implicit or Hybrid response type
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Prioritization

If this issue is not closed in time for KC 26.4, the list of supported standards in needs to revert the changes in #41348 from "FAPI 2.0 Security Profile" to "FAPI 2.0 Security Profile (Draft)".
To make this dependency visible, this is now marked as a blocker. 
It can be downgraded to "important" if the PR above is reverted.

### Describe the bug

After configureing to apply RejectImplicitGrantExecutor to a client's request, when the client sent a PAR request that includes  Implicit or Hybrid response type (other than "respose_type=code"), Keycloak returns a successful response in 201 status code.

After that, when the client send an authorization request consecutively, Keycloak returns an error response.

Indeed, Keycloak returns an error response from authorization endpoint finally, but Keycloak should return an error response from PAR endpoint in 400 status code firstly, which can omit sending an unnecessary authorization request.

### Version

26.3.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

1. After configureing to apply RejectImplicitGrantExecutor to a client's request, the client sent a PAR request that includes Implicit or Hybrid response type (other than "respose_type=code". e.g., "response_type=token").
2. keycloak returns an error response in 400 status code with "invalid_request" error.

### Actual behavior

1. After configureing to apply RejectImplicitGrantExecutor to a client's request, the client sent a PAR request that includes Implicit or Hybrid response type (other than "respose_type=code". e.g., "response_type=token").
2. keycloak returns an error response in 201status code with valid "request_uri" parameter.

### How to Reproduce?

1. After configureing to apply RejectImplicitGrantExecutor to a client's request, the client sent a PAR request that includes Implicit or Hybrid response type (other than "respose_type=code". e.g., "response_type=token").

### Anything else?

The OpenID Foundation's conformance suite of FAPI 2.0 Security Profile Final specification expects the expected behavior.

issue_title: Test SMTP connection fails when no port is specified
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

This is a regression created by #39486. When no port is specified the condition added for that issue incorrectly compares the empty string `""` with the port `25`. Check this [line](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/26.3.2/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/RealmAdminResource.java). Therefore the test thinks that the server is different (different port) and the password is not recovered from the configuration. This finishes with a login error instead of a successful test.

The root cause seems to be that the `port` is sent by the console as `null` (in json `"port": null`) and that makes `settings.getOrDefault("port", "25")` return `null`. So I will use Optional or similar.

The easy workaround is just specify the default port to `25` in the admin console.

### Version

26.3.2

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The test should work OK.

### Actual behavior

Test fails with incorrect password as the password is not recovered from the configuration.

### How to Reproduce?

Just configure a SMTP server in default port 25 without specifying it in teh configuration. After saving , reload the smtp config and test again. It will fail with invalid credentials.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: TiDB Many WAITING threads during high load scenario
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area core ### Describe the bug I am currently testing our KC to see how much load it can handle in the current configuration. Running on EKS using 3 dedicated c7i.xlarge machines Because some DaemonSets are running also there, this is the KC resource config: ``` resources: limits: cpu: "3" memory: 6G requests: cpu: "3" memory: 6G ``` Its a KC 26.2.5 instance database connections ``` - name: KC_DB_POOL_INITIAL_SIZE value: "25" - name: KC_DB_POOL_MAX_SIZE value: "25" - name: KC_DB_POOL_MIN_SIZE value: "25" ``` ``` KC_HTTP_MAX_QUEUED_REQUESTS=1000 KC_HTTP_POOL_MAX_THREADS=50 ``` 230 http req/sec are generated (~ 30 Logins/sec, ~30 code2token/sec,~150 refreshtoken/sec) via the Keycloak benchmark cli. It is strange that with this number of requests, the response times increase, even though the CPU of the pod is only at 80%. The database connection pool is only utilized at 15%. It seems that the requests are spending a long time in the WorkerPool Queue (since the usage duration values are very low, and queue duration values are high): That's why I increased the KC_HTTP_POOL_MAX_THREADS, unfortunately without success. Then I looked into Threads with JavaMissionControl. Seems to be that all WAITING threads are blocked by this line of code: [https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/73e5f4bead7840f19210aaabcbd1a00895d46929[…]oak/models/sessions/infinispan/changes/JpaChangesPerformer.java](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/73e5f4bead7840f19210aaabcbd1a00895d46929/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/sessions/infinispan/changes/JpaChangesPerformer.java#L109C23-L109C59) Furthermore I saw that the vert.x threads are under heavy load. Is that the newly introduced virtual threads feature for infinispan/jgroups? Any Ideas? ### Version KC 26.2.5 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior CPU can be fully utilized ### Actual behavior CPU is not fully utilized, but threads are waiting on each other ### How to Reproduce? Run Keycloak Benchmark up to 250req/sec with scenario `keycloak.scenario.authentication.AuthorizationCode` on 3x 3CPU/6GB RAM KC Cluster ### Anything else? _No response_

issue_title: Keycloak SAML Adapter subsystem does not work in WildFly 37
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

adapter/jee-saml

### Describe the bug

Attempting to use the latest Keycloak SAML adapter with the [newly released WildFly 37](https://www.wildfly.org/news/2025/08/04/WildFly-37-is-released/) results in a server start failure due to a missing constructor exception.

`Caused by: java.lang.NoSuchMethodError: 'void org.jboss.as.server.deployment.module.ModuleDependency.<init>(org.jboss.modules.ModuleLoader, org.jboss.modules.ModuleIdentifier, boolean, boolean, boolean, boolean)'
  at org.keycloak.keycloak-saml-wildfly-subsystem@26.3.2//org.keycloak.subsystem.adapter.saml.extension.KeycloakDependencyProcessorWildFly.addCoreModules(KeycloakDependencyProcessorWildFly.java:41)
  at org.keycloak.keycloak-saml-wildfly-subsystem@26.3.2//org.keycloak.subsystem.adapter.saml.extension.KeycloakDependencyProcessor.deploy(KeycloakDependencyProcessor.java:71)
  at org.jboss.as.server@29.0.0.Final//org.jboss.as.server.deployment.DeploymentUnitPhaseService.start(DeploymentUnitPhaseService.java:165)`

Upon investigation, the Keycloak adapter currently relies on deprecated & marked for removal features from WildFly core, some of which were removed in the latest WildFly core release, 29.0.0.Final. Namely, `org.jboss.modules.ModuleIdentifier` and the `org.jboss.as.server.deployment.module.ModuleDependency` constructor in use in `KeycloakDependencyProcessor `& `KeycloakDependencyProcessorWildFly` with in the `keycloak-saml-wildfly-subsystem` jar.

### Version

SAML Adapter 26.3.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A SAML adapter configured WildFly instance to start successfully and allow for successful SAML authentication for the deployed & configured applications.

### Actual behavior

WildFly fails to start the SAML adapter and therefore, fails to start the whole server.

### How to Reproduce?

- Configure WildFly 37 with the latest SAML adapter, e.g. through installing the feature pack using galleon
- Configure & enable the keycloak-saml subsystem and elytron as required
- Attempt to start the server

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Updated the WildFly subsystem module to avoid using deprecated functionality from wildfly-core that's been removed in the latest version

Replaced instances of the deprecated class ModuleIdentifier with strings.
For now, maintain the use of the deprecated ModuleDependency constructor (while now using the module identifier strings from above in place of the ModuleIdentifier instances) in order to maximise compatibility. This ensures support for both WildFly, up to at least 37, and EAP 8.
Moved all the module identifier strings to KeycloakDependencyProcessor so they're all in one place. Removed duplicates and update references from other classes.

Closes #41669

### Before reporting an issue

### Area

### Describe the bug

To follow up these issues https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/41670, https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/41459, I've now configured on the brokered OP the `claims` parameter as a forwarded query parameter.

issue_title: Authorization filtering causes NullPointerException with "Null keys are not supported!" in searchForUserStream (26.3.1+)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

account/api

### Describe the bug

Starting in Keycloak 26.3.1, any call to `searchForUserStream()` in custom providers triggers a `NullPointerException: Null keys are not supported!` due to authorization filtering attempting to lookup users with null IDs.

## Environment
- **Keycloak Version**: 26.3.1, 26.3.2 (confirmed broken in both)
- **Java Version**: 21
- **Database**: PostgreSQL


## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a custom `RealmResourceProvider`
2. Call `userQueryProvider.searchForUserStream(realm, searchParams)` with any search parameters
3. The call immediately fails

### Version

26.3.2

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

`searchForUserStream()` should return users matching the search criteria without throwing exceptions

### Actual behavior

## Error details:

```
java.lang.NullPointerException: Null keys are not supported!
    at org.infinispan.commons.util.Util.checkNotNull(Util.java:136)
    at org.infinispan.cache.impl.CacheImpl.get(CacheImpl.java:401)
    at org.keycloak.models.cache.infinispan.UserCacheSession.getUserById(UserCacheSession.java:XXX)
    at org.keycloak.services.DefaultKeycloakContext.getUser(DefaultKeycloakContext.java:XXX)
    at org.keycloak.authorization.fgap.evaluation.partial.PartialEvaluator.getPredicates(PartialEvaluator.java:61)
    at org.keycloak.authorization.fgap.AdminPermissionsSchema.applyAuthorizationFilters(AdminPermissionsSchema.java:452)
```

### How to Reproduce?

## This will fail in 26.3.1+
Stream<UserModel> users = session.users().searchForUserStream(realm, Map.of());
return Response.ok(users.count()).build();

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: OIDC identity provider token refresh fails with JsonMapperException
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

Token refresh with identity brokering fails with JsonMapperException. I did some debugging, and it seems the root cause is that refreshExpiresIn is null. In my integration scenario, i use PING Federate. It seems PING is not providing refresh_expires_in with a token refresh response. According to spec, this is allowed. 

### Version

nightly from 12.08.2025

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

In AbstractOAuth2IdentityProvider.refreshToken, please take over the refreshExpiresIn from previousResponse, as you did for the id token. That should solve the issue. 

### Actual behavior

OIDC id broker token refresh fails with JsonMapperException

### How to Reproduce?

Integrate Keycloak with a OIDC provider that is not providing refresh_expires_in with token refresh response, e.g. PING Federate

### Anything else?

It is my first bug report. Hope it is provided with sufficient details.
commit_message:
Make sure refresh expiration is set together with setting the previous refresh token

issue_title: CVE-2025-7962 In Jakarta Mail 2.0.2 it is possible to preform a SMTP Injection by utilizing the \r and \n UTF-8 characters to separate different messages
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

It seems that we are affected by https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-7962 / https://access.redhat.com/security/cve/cve-2025-48924. It is explained here https://gitlab.eclipse.org/security/cve-assignement/-/issues/67 that this also affects angus implementation in version 2.0.3. So we need to upgrade to 2.0.4 that fixed the issue in this commit https://github.com/eclipse-ee4j/angus-mail/commit/84fe702f7a4837f325b878f17fee148a7e8ba951.

A flaw was found in com.sun.mail/jakarta.mail. The jakarta.mail component allows an attacker to inject SMTP messages by exploiting improper handling of carriage return and newline characters encoded in UTF-8. An unauthenticated attacker can leverage this vulnerability to send arbitrary SMTP messages. This injection occurs via crafted email content, potentially leading to unauthorized message transmission.

Low severity.


### Version

26.3.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Upgrade to angus 2.0.4.

### Actual behavior

we are in angus 2.0.3 which is affected by that CVE.

### How to Reproduce?

N/A
